title: Interactive Density Graphing MicroSim
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Chemistry
description: Students plot mass vs. volume data, calculate the slope of the best-fit line, and match the resulting density to known materials.

learningObjectives: Plot experimental mass vs. volume data points on a scatter plot with proper units.
Use a line of best fit to determine the density (slope) of a mystery substance.
Compare measured densities with a reference table to identify unknown materials.
prerequisites: Understands mass, volume, and density relationships
Can read scales on scatter plots and apply linear models
standards: NGSS HS-PS1-2: Construct and revise explanations for macroscopic properties using particle-level models.
Science Practice: Analyze data using tools, technologies, and models.

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive data exploration and slope interpretation
keyQuestions: How does the slope of a mass vs. volume graph reveal density?
Which materials have densities closest to the calculated slope?
commonMisconceptions: Believing density depends on sample size rather than mass/volume ratio.
Confusing axes (mass on x-axis instead of y-axis).
assessmentOpportunities: Students justify the identified material using numerical evidence.
Monitor accuracy of plotted points and discussion of slope differences.
